items.title: Tim Picton's accused killer pleads not guilty to manslaughter

children.content: The man accused of killing former Labor identity Tim Picton outside a Northbridge venue has pleaded not guilty to his manslaughter.

items.title: More women come forward after sepsis case linked to pedicure

items.title: Australia's richest woman gifts plane to Pauline Hanson

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

image.altText: A woman wearing whtie pants and red and white shirts steps onto the wing of a grey plane.

items.title: Experts offer theories on why WA is 'wild west' for ADHD prescriptions

children.content: With WA home to seven out of Australia's top-10 highest ADHD prescribing neighbourhoods, experts, psychiatrists and doctors weigh in with their theories.

image.altText: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) medications Concerta, Vyvanse and Ritalin on a pharmacy counter.

items.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain

children.content: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

image.altText: Man checks his rain gauge

items.title: Ultra-swimmer sets world record with 55km solo feat in crocodile-filled river

children.content: Andy Donaldson took less than 12 hours to swim the challenging 55-kilometre course down the Upper Ord River in Western Australia's north.

items.title: Does the gas industry pay its fair share of tax?

children.content: Advocates are proposing a flat 25 per cent tax on gas exports, but representatives say the industry is being unfairly targeted.

image.altText: An aerial shot of the Gorgon gas project on the coast of Western Australia.

items.title: Monitor appointed to address tensions between councillors at Perth shire

children.content: A fifth local authority in WA has been assigned an independent monitor, this time at the council's request, to focus on council member conduct and improving relationships.

image.altText: Row of six men in dark blue suits with one woman in a red pantsuit.

items.title: Woman and child allegedly carjacked after burning bike blocks road

children.content: Police allege Spencer Thorne held a woman and her two-year-old child hostage after carjacking them in Dalyellup, south of Bunbury, in WA's South West.

items.title: Perth man pleads not guilty to father's murder on grounds of insanity

children.content: David Moussa, 32, pleads not guilty on grounds of insanity to murdering his father in the Perth suburb of Clarkson.

image.altText: Police tape cordons off a street with police vans parked on either side of the road and an officer walking across the street.

items.title: Ex-Eagles skipper Allen out for three months at Lions with foot injury

children.content: The Lions' win over the Crows on Sunday has come at a price, with one of their key off-season signings rule out for much of the season.

children.content: Bunbury fisherman Mason Jones, who led a protest against the WA government's demersal fishing ban, says he is willing to go to jail to keep campaigning against the measures.

children.content: The West Australian government has announced it plans to build 500 homes for public servants in regional areas over five years despite a "critical worker shortage".

items.title: King's speech to Congress hits on several Trump controversies

children.content: In a speech that was supposed to be apolitical, King Charles hits on some hot-button issues that are fuelling controversies around the Trump administration.

image.altText: King Charles speaks while JD Vance and Mike Johnson applaud in the background.

items.title: Police honour Kalgoorlie detectives brutally killed 100 years ago

children.content: On this day in 1926, Detective Inspector John Walsh and Detective Sergeant Alexander Pitman were shot while trying to apprehend gold thieves near Kalgoorlie. Their charred and dismembered bodies were later found at the bottom of a mine shaft.

A man and a police officer holding a wreath at a memorial service.

items.title: Childcare group G8 to close 10pc of centres after child abuse scandal

children.content: About 36,000 children are enrolled under the brand's 395 childcare and early learning centres, which include centres such as Headstart, Jellybeans, Kool Kids and Kindy Patch Kids.

image.altText: An image of a young childs hand playing with plastic letters to spell the word goat.

items.title: Evidence of 25-million-year-old platypuses with teeth found in outback

children.content: Researchers have discovered ancient platypus fossils in outback South Australia, where they are believed to have lived with lungfish, flamingos and freshwater dolphins. They also had major a difference from the modern species: well-formed teeth.

image.altText: An artwork showing a brown platypus in green water

items.title: Tigers, Eagles or Cockle Divers — what makes up a football club's name?

children.content: How have Australia's football clubs come to be named, and what do those names represent? We analysed over 1,500 senior Aussie Rules clubs to find the patterns in how clubs have named themselves.

items.title: Former ASIO chief raised questions about assessment of Bondi gunman

items.title: Family says grandmother's death after weight-loss surgery preventable

items.title: Don't assume El Niño warming will bring record heat

items.title: AI will be 'the biggest challenge of all' for workplaces if it's not controlled
